talks.title: Clustering and mapping text data
talks.desc: Hands-on session using large language model text embeddings to cluster and map NICAR conference sessions, and how reporters can adapt the workflow for their own text datasets.

talks.title: Search and News
talks.desc: Panel on how AI search and chatbots are changing how people find news, what that means for traffic and attribution, and how journalists should respond.

talks.title: Navigating the shift to generative search
talks.desc: With the rise of AI-driven search tools, news publishers face fundamentally changed models of distribution, attribution, and engagement. This session with Columbia's Tow Center shares research on critical vulnerabilities in generative search technologies and discusses how publishers and journalists can monitor and control how their content is being used.

talks.title: OA, Attribution, and Addressing Faculty AI Concerns
talks.desc: Webcast for librarians and faculty on how open access and attribution work in the age of AI tools, and how to address common concerns about reuse of scholarship.

talks.org: American Association for Public Opinion Research (AAPOR)
talks.title: Can LLMs Extract Transparency Metrics from Text Descriptions of Polling Methods
talks.desc: Research presentation on whether large language models can reliably extract methodological transparency metrics from poll descriptions at scale.

talks.title: Using Large Language Models to Help Newsrooms Aggregate Polls For the 2024 Election
talks.desc: Talk on PollFinder.ai, a tool that uses LLMs to help newsrooms find, standardize, and track election polling across outlets.

talks.title: Editing AI-powered Journalism
talks.desc: Panel discussion with editors on how AI is being used in newsrooms today, what's worked, and what they've learned about editing AI-assisted journalism.
